assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Regular channel will be force-upgraded to the next minor version (1.31) when 1.30 hits EoS
assertions.reason: Response clearly states Regular channel clusters will be 'Auto-upgraded immediately' to 'the oldest supported version in Regular channel (likely 1.31.x)' with 'No grace period'

assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Extended channel get up to 24 months of support, so 1.30 EoS is significantly later — they have more time
assertions.reason: Response mentions Extended channel has longer support than Regular channel but doesn't specify the 24-month timeframe or explain that their EoS date is significantly later

assertions.expectation: Explains that legacy 'No channel' clusters: control plane EoS minor versions are auto-upgraded to next supported minor, AND EoS node pools are force-upgraded even when 'no auto-upgrade' is configured
assertions.reason: Response incorrectly states No channel clusters have 'No automatic upgrades' and 'will remain on 1.30', missing the critical detail that both control plane and node pools are force-upgraded at EoS

assertions.expectation: Mentions that a 'no upgrades' maintenance exclusion (30 days) can temporarily defer even EoS force-upgrades
assertions.reason: Response does not mention maintenance exclusion windows as a way to temporarily defer EoS force-upgrades

assertions.expectation: Recommends proactive upgrade planning rather than waiting for EoS enforcement
assertions.reason: Response strongly recommends proactive manual upgrades as 'Option 1' and emphasizes upgrading before EoS with detailed preparation steps

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE EoS notifications via Cloud Logging as a planning tool
assertions.reason: Response does not mention Cloud Logging notifications for EoS planning

assertions.expectation: Mentions the deprecation insights dashboard for checking API compatibility before the forced upgrade
assertions.reason: Response mentions checking deprecated APIs via kubectl command but does not specifically mention the deprecation insights dashboard

assertions.expectation: Warns that 'snowflaking' (manually freezing versions) should be avoided — it creates security and reliability risks
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'Increased security risk' for No channel clusters but doesn't specifically use the term 'snowflaking' or warn against manually freezing versions as a general practice
